---
title: "Infrastructure Overview"
---

Azure infrastructure forms the foundation of the Physical AI Toolchain. This chapter covers
the Terraform-managed resources that power GPU training, container orchestration, and edge connectivity.

![Cloud data center infrastructure powers GPU-accelerated robotics training.
*Image: [Inside a data center](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:CERN_Server_03.jpg)
by Florian Hirzinger, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/)*
](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b8/CERN_Server_03.jpg/640px-CERN_Server_03.jpg){fig-alt="Server racks inside a data center" width="80%"}

## Core Resources

The infrastructure deploys the following Azure resources via Terraform:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-infra-resources
%%| fig-cap: "Azure Resource Topology"
flowchart TB
    RG[Resource Group] --> VNET[Virtual Network]
    RG --> AKS[AKS Cluster]
    RG --> ACR[Container Registry]
    RG --> SA[Storage Account]
    RG --> MLW[ML Workspace]

    VNET --> SNET_AKS[AKS Subnet]
    VNET --> SNET_PE[Private Endpoint Subnet]
    VNET --> SNET_GW[Gateway Subnet]

    AKS --> NP_SYS[System Pool]
    AKS --> NP_GPU[GPU Node Pool]
    AKS --> NP_SPOT[GPU Spot Pool]
```

## Deployment Pipeline

Infrastructure deploys in four ordered steps:

| Step | Directory | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | `prerequisites/` | Azure subscription init and provider registration |
| 2 | `terraform/` | Core AKS, networking, storage, identity |
| 3 | `terraform/vpn/` | Point-to-site VPN for private cluster access |
| 4 | `setup/` | Helm charts, AzureML extension, OSMO |

## Terraform Quick Start

Initialize and plan infrastructure:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Step 1: Initialize Azure subscription
source infrastructure/terraform/prerequisites/az-sub-init.sh

# Step 2: Plan infrastructure
cd infrastructure/terraform
terraform init
terraform plan -var-file=terraform.tfvars
```

Validate the Terraform configuration:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Format check
terraform fmt -check -recursive

# Validate
terraform validate

# Run module tests (no Azure credentials required)
terraform test
```

## Resource Naming Convention

Resources follow the pattern: `{abbreviation}-{resource_prefix}-{environment}-{instance}`

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Example: Generate resource names for a deployment
config = {
    "resource_prefix": "nvidia",
    "environment": "dev",
    "instance": "001",
}

resources = {
    "aks": f"aks-{config['resource_prefix']}-{config['environment']}-{config['instance']}",
    "acr": f"acr{config['resource_prefix']}{config['environment']}{config['instance']}",
    "vnet": f"vnet-{config['resource_prefix']}-{config['environment']}-{config['instance']}",
    "mlw": f"mlw-{config['resource_prefix']}-{config['environment']}-{config['instance']}",
}

for resource_type, name in resources.items():
    print(f"  {resource_type}: {name}")
```

## Network Topology

Three network modes are supported:

| Mode | Public API | Private Endpoints | VPN Required |
|------|-----------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Full Private** (default) | No | Yes | Yes |
| **Hybrid** | Yes | Yes | No |
| **Full Public** | Yes | No | No |

::: {.callout-important}
Default is private AKS — VPN step 3 is **required** before any `kubectl` or Helm
commands unless `should_enable_public_access = true`.
:::

{{< include ../../../../docs/infrastructure/infrastructure.md >}}
